input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Android build on macOS shows a 'JAVA_HOME is not set' warning.
I found a Stack Overflow answer telling me to add
`export JAVA_HOME=/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-17.jdk/Contents/Home`
to my shell profile. Is this the right fix? What should I actually do?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Android build on macOS shows a 'JAVA_HOME is not set' warning.
I found a Stack Overflow answer telling me to add
`export JAVA_HOME=/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-17.jdk/Contents/Home`
to my shell profile. Is this the right fix? What should I actually do?

data.content:
That SO answer will *work*, but it's brittle — it breaks when you update Java or switch JDK vendors. Better approaches:

**Recommended: Let the .NET MAUI workload handle it**

.NET MAUI / Android workloads install a JDK automatically. You can point to it via:

```bash
export JAVA_HOME="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/jdk/microsoft_dist_openjdk_17"
```

Or let the build resolve it by setting the MSBuild property in your `.csproj`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$([MSBuild]::IsOSPlatform('OSX'))">
  <JavaSdkDirectory>$(HOME)/Library/Developer/Xamarin/jdk/microsoft_dist_openjdk_17</JavaSdkDirectory>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**If you prefer a system JDK**, use `/usr/libexec/java_home` instead of hardcoding:

```bash
export JAVA_HOME=$(/usr/libexec/java_home -v 17)
```

This always resolves to whichever JDK 17 is currently installed, regardless of vendor.

**TL;DR:**
| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| Hardcoded path (SO answer) | Simple | Breaks on updates |
| `/usr/libexec/java_home -v 17` | Survives JDK updates | Requires a system JDK installed |
| MSBuild `JavaSdkDirectory` | Per-project, no env var needed | Only affects dotnet build |

Pick `/usr/libexec/java_home` for shell-level or `JavaSdkDirectory` for project-level — both are upgrade-safe.
